title: Mudumalai National Park
tagline: Mudumalai National Park — wildlife reserve of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Mudumalai National Park is a national park in the Nilgiri Mountains in Tamil Nadu in southern India.
overview.description: Mudumalai National Park is a national park in the Nilgiri Mountains in Tamil Nadu in southern India. It covers 321 km2 (124 sq mi) at an elevation range of 850–1,250 m (2,790–4,100 ft) in the Nilgiri District and shares boundaries with the states of Karnataka and Kerala. A part of this area has been protected since 1940. The national park has been part of Nilgiri Biosphere Reserve since 1986 and was declared a tiger reserve together with a buffer zone of 367.59 km2 (141.93 sq mi) in 2007.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Manavayal
topPlaces.description: Manavayal is a village in the Pattukkottai taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Devarshola
topPlaces.description: Devarshola is a panchayat town in The Nilgiris district in the state of Tamil Nadu, India.Devarshola comes under the administration of Gudalur revenue division and the taluk of Gudalur. It is one of the major tourist attractions in gudalur forest division and nearby to Mudumalai, Bandipur and Wayanad National parks.

topPlaces.name: Theppakadu
topPlaces.description: Theppakadu is a village in the Nilgiris district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. As per Census 2011, it is part of the Mudumalai village which covers 188.57 km2 (72.81 sq mi) and had a population of 1,694 persons in 2011.

topPlaces.name: Chembakolli
topPlaces.description: Chembakolli is a village in the south of India, in the Nilgiris district, Tamil Nadu. It is located in the Nilgiri Hills.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Mudumalai National Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Mudumalai National Park is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Mudumalai National Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mysuru Airport (72 km) and the nearest railway station is Mysuru Junction (80 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Mudumalai National Park?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Mudumalai National Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Mudumalai National Park?
faq.a: Stays in Mudumalai National Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Mudumalai National Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Manavayal, Devarshola, Theppakadu, Chembakolli.
